title: NA-Non ULIP
word_text:
Factors That Affect Term Life Insurance Premiums
Life insurance premium is the cost of buying the policy. It can be paid in one lump sum ( single premium), for a limited period (limited premium), or throughout the chosen policy tenure ( regular premium). Moreover, insurance companies offer different premium paying frequencies like annual, half-yearly, quarterly and monthly.
The premium calculation is done by the insurance company and it depends on different factors. let’s assess the factors that affect life insurance premiums.
Do Premiums Increase Every Year?
In India, increasing the premium for insurance every year is not a rule. However, the term insurance rate increase depends on several factors:
Renewable Term Insurance Plans: The increase in premiums is possible when you have decided to opt for a renewable term plan. It is because insurers re-analyze your age and risk level at every renewal.
Health Changes: Premium can also go up but if there is a sudden change in your health condition during renewal. This happens when insurers review your medical updates before continuing the coverage.
End of Initial Period: When you are in the initial years of the policy, there are no changes in the premiums. But for some premiums, at the end of the period, there can be an increase on the new rate structure.
Inflation: The inflation can also impact the premium prices over time. This happens to keep in range with the rising medical or life coverage costs.
Riders and Add-Ons: Your premium charges also increase when you have added extra features like critical illness or accidental cover increases the overall premium amount.
What Are the Factors That Affect Term Insurance Premium?
The different factors that affect term insurance premiums and their respective effects are detailed below –
1. Life expectancy
Life expectancy means for how long the insured individual is expected to live. If the life expectancy is higher, it means that the insured has lower chances of premature death. In such cases, the premiums are lower.
2. Add-on benefits and features
Add-on features and benefits include inbuilt riders, additional coverage options, whole life coverage options, return of premium option, etc. If a term insurance plan offers these add-on benefits, it has a wider scope of coverage. A higher premium is charged for this wider coverage. Moreover, if you choose additional coverage features like whole life coverage, return of premium benefit, etc., the premium goes up.
3. Age
Age is one of the most important factor affecting life insurance premium. This is because age affects your mortality risk. With rising age, the body grows weak and diseases set in. Moreover, as you get older, your mortality risk increases. Thus, term insurance premiums are usually higher in older ages compared to younger ages.
4. Occupation
Some occupations are dangerous and increase the mortality risk. Common examples include mining, aviation, politics, defence, etc. Premiums for individuals engaged in such risky occupations are higher.
5. Health condition
If you have adverse health conditions like diabetes, hypertension, etc., your health risk increases and so does the mortality risk. As such, higher premiums are charged in such cases4.
6. Smoking habit
Term insurance premiums are higher for individuals who smoke5. This is because smoking increases health hazards and the mortality risk. Alternatively, non-smokers can enjoy lower premium rates since they are considered healthy.
7. Lifestyle habits
If you have bad lifestyle habits like drinking or substance abuse, your term insurance premiums would rise.
8. Tenure of the plan
Annual premiums are lower for plans which are long-term than for plans which have a shorter tenure.
9. Personal and family medical history
If you have adverse medical history in your family or if you have previously suffered from any medical complication, the premiums would be higher.
10. Build
Your height and weight also affect term insurance premiums as these parameters determine your BMI. A higher BMI indicates unhealthy lifestyle and premiums are higher in such cases.
11. Riders
If you add riders to the term insurance policy, you have to pay an extra premium for each rider chosen. This increases the overall premium of the term insurance policy.
Why Are Companies Hiking Term Insurance Premium Rates?
In the past few years, it has been observed that term insurance premium rates in the country have increased. But why did this happen? Here are some factors to look into:
Post-COVID Mortality Impact: After the COVID pandemic, there have been higher-than-expected death claims. This has led to an increased risk overall which in turn forces the insurers to make changes in their pricing structure over time.
Reinsurance Price Hikes: Post-COVID, reinsurers raised their charges due to rising mortality and uncertainties, which directly impacted term plan pricing.
Tighter Underwriting Norms: As the claims have increased, insurers have also become more cautious. The premium costs have been a result of stricter medical evaluations and risk assessments.
Market Competition and Sustainability: To remain competitive, insurers are adjusting prices. While still affordable, premiums are now more aligned with current health and risk realities.
How to Reduce Term Insurance Premiums?
There are ways in which you can reduce term insurance premiums. Some such ways are as follows -
1. Purchase a term insurance policy at young age
As mentioned earlier, premiums are lower at younger ages and higher at higher ages2. As such, when you buy a policy at a young age, you can get lower premiums which also remain the same throughout the policy tenure.
2. Opt for a longer policy duration
As the policy duration increases, the annual premium reduces. So, if you choose a higher tenure, your premium would come down.
3. Look for rebates
Term insurance plans offer rebates which discount the premium. Some of the commonly available rebates include the following–
o Sum assured rebate
This rebate is offered if you choose a high sum assured. The rebate usually increases with the sum assured chosen.
o Modal rebate
This rebate is offered for paying the premium in the annual or half-yearly modes. Monthly and quarterly modes might attract additional premiums but annual and half-yearly modes allow discounts.
Moreover, under some plans you might also find lower premiums for females.
4. Evaluate plans before purchasing
There are different types of term insurance plans available in the market. Each plan is priced differently. So, if you compare and then choose a suitable plan, you might enjoy affordable premiums.
How to Avoid Rising Premium Costs?
Rising insurance premiums can impact your long-term financial planning. Here are some smart ways to keep your costs in check:
Select a Long-Term Fixed Premium
Choose a term insurance plan with fixed premiums throughout the policy term. This locks your premium at the entry rate, protecting you from future hikes.
Check Your Coverage Frequently
Review your policy every few years. You might be paying for more coverage than needed or missing better options in the market.
Improve Your Health
Maintain a healthy lifestyle. Non-smokers, fit individuals, and those without pre-existing health conditions often get lower premium rates.
Compare Different Policies
Always compare various policies and then only make your final decision. You can compare various plans as per your requirements.
How to Choose the Right Term Insurance Plan?
Choosing the right term insurance plan ensures financial security for your family. Here’s what to consider:
Adequate Coverage
Choose an assured sum that covers your family’s future needs, debts, and living expenses. A good rule is 10–15 times your annual income.
Affordable Premium
Select a plan with premiums that fit your budget while offering sufficient coverage. Avoid underinsuring just to save money.
Riders and Add-Ons
Look for essential riders like critical illness, accidental death, or waiver of premium to enhance protection.
Policy Term
Pick a term that aligns with your retirement age or until major financial responsibilities are met.
Insurer’s Credibility
Check the insurance company’s claim settlement ratio and customer reviews for reliability and support.
Making an informed choice gives you peace of mind and financial security for your loved ones.
Conclusion
Term insurance plans have low premiums which are affected by various factors. Know these factors so that you know how the premium is calculated. Ensure that the premium is affordable so that you can continue paying it over the chosen tenure and enjoy complete protection under the term insurance plan.
FAQs
What is the term insurance premium, and why does it vary?
A term insurance premium is the amount you pay regularly to keep your life insurance plan active. It varies based on your age, health, lifestyle, policy duration, and the coverage amount you choose. Simply put, the higher the risk to the insurer, the higher the premium you may need to pay.
How does age impact term insurance premiums?
Age is a major factor in determining your premium. The younger you are when you buy term insurance, the lower your premium. This is because younger people are generally healthier and pose a lower risk to the insurer.
Can I change the coverage amount to adjust the premium?
Yes, you can. Increasing the coverage amount will raise your premium, while reducing it can lower your premium. But make sure the coverage is still enough to secure your family's financial needs.
How does the policy duration affect the premium?
Longer policy terms often have slightly higher premiums, but they offer protection for a longer time. Shorter terms may cost less, but could leave you without coverage when you need it later in life.
5. What are some of the factors that affect life insurance premiums?
Premiums depend on various factors like your age, gender, health status, lifestyle habits (such as smoking), occupation, sum assured, policy term, and any added riders. Each of these influences the overall risk for the insurer.
6. What does the term insurance premium depend on?
Term insurance premium is calculated based on your profile (age, health, habits), the coverage amount, duration of the plan, and extra benefits or riders you choose. The higher the risk, the more you will pay.
7.  Does the term insurance premium rise over time?
No, the term insurance premium is fixed once you buy the policy. Thereafter, it does not rise over time but remains constant
Disclaimer:
The views stated in this article are not to be construed as investment advice and readers are suggested to seek independent financial advice before making any investment decisions. full_pdf_text:
Factors That Affect Term Life Insurance Premiums
Life insurance premium is the cost of buying the policy. It can be paid in one lump sum ( single
premium), for a limited period (limited premium), or throughout the chosen policy tenure (
regular premium). Moreover, insurance companies offer different premium paying frequencies
like annual, half-yearly, quarterly and monthly.
The premium calculation is done by the insurance company and it depends on different factors.
let’s assess the factors that affect life insurance premiums.
Do Premiums Increase Every Year?
In India, increasing the premium for insurance every year is not a rule. However, the term insurance rate increase depends on several factors:
● Renewable Term Insurance Plans: The increase in premiums is possible when you have decided to opt for a renewable term plan. It is because insurers re-analyze your age and risk level at every renewal.
● Health Changes: Premium can also go up but if there is a sudden change in your health condition during renewal. This happens when insurers review your medical updates before continuing the coverage.
● Inflation: The inflation can also impact the premium prices over time. This happens to keep in range with the rising medical or life coverage costs.
● Riders and Add-Ons: Your premium charges also increase when you have added coverage via riders such as critical illness benefit rider or accidental death benefit rider which increases the overall premium amount .
What Are the Factors That Affect Term Insurance Premium?
The different factors that affect term insurance premiums and their respective effects are
detailed below –
1. Life expectancy
Life expectancy means for how long the insured individual is expected to live. If the life
expectancy is higher, it means that the insured has lower chances of premature death. In such
cases, the premiums are lower.
2. Add-on benefits and features
Add-on features and benefits include inbuilt riders, additional coverage options, whole life
coverage options, return of premium option, etc. If a term insurance plan offers these add-on
benefits, it has a wider scope of coverage. A higher premium is charged for this wider coverage.
Moreover, if you choose additional coverage features like whole life coverage, return of premium
benefit, etc., the premium goes up.
3. Age
Age is one of the most important factor affecting life insurance premium. This is because age
affects your mortality risk. With rising age, the body grows weak and diseases set in. Moreover,
as you get older, your mortality risk increases. Thus, term insurance premiums are usually higher
in older ages compared to younger ages.
4. Occupation
Some occupations are dangerous and increase the mortality risk. Common examples include
mining, aviation, politics, defence, etc. Premiums for individuals engaged in such risky
occupations are higher.
5. Health condition
If you have adverse health conditions like diabetes, hypertension, etc., your health risk increases
and so does the mortality risk. As such, higher premiums are charged in such cases4.
6. Smoking habit
Term insurance premiums are higher for individuals who smoke5. This is because smoking
increases health hazards and the mortality risk. Alternatively, non-smokers can enjoy lower
premium rates since they are considered healthy.
7. Lifestyle habits
If you have bad lifestyle habits like drinking or substance abuse, your term insurance premiums
would rise.
8. Tenure of the plan
Annual premiums are lower for plans which are long-term than for plans which have a shorter
tenure.
9. Personal and family medical history
If you have adverse medical history in your family or if you have previously suffered from any
medical complication, the premiums would be higher.
10. Build
Your height and weight also affect term insurance premiums as these parameters determine your
BMI. A higher BMI indicates unhealthy lifestyle and premiums are higher in such cases.
11. Riders
If you add riders to the term insurance policy, you have to pay an extra premium for each rider
chosen. This increases the overall premium of the term insurance policy.
Understanding the Rise in Term Insurance Premiums
You may have noticed a slight increase in term insurance premiums recently. This change helps insurers offer more sustainable, reliable, and long-term protection by factoring in updated health data, risk trends, and global developments. Here’s what’s driving the change.
● Post-COVID Mortality Impact: After the COVID pandemic, there has been a reduced mortality which has resulted in higher-than-expected death claims. This has led to an increased risk overall which in turn forces the insurers to make changes in their pricing structure over time.
● Reinsurance Price Hikes: Post-COVID, reinsurers raised their charges due to rising mortality and uncertainties, which directly impacted term plan pricing.
● Tighter Underwriting Norms: As the claims have increased, insurers have also become more cautious. The premium costs have been a result of stricter medical evaluations and risk assessments.
● Market Competition and Sustainability: To remain competitive, insurers are adjusting prices. While still affordable, premiums are now more aligned with current health and risk realities.
How to Reduce Term Insurance Premiums?
There are ways in which you can reduce term insurance premiums. Some such ways are as follows
-
1. Purchase a term insurance policy at young age
As mentioned earlier, premiums are lower at younger ages and higher at higher ages2. As such,
when you buy a policy at a young age, you can get lower premiums which also remain the same
throughout the policy tenure.
2. Opt for a longer policy duration
As the policy duration increases, the annual premium reduces. So, if you choose a higher tenure,
your premium would come down.
3. Look for rebates
Term insurance plans offer rebates which discount the premium. Some of the commonly
available rebates include the following–
o Sum assured rebate
This rebate is offered if you choose a high sum assured. The rebate usually increases with the
sum assured chosen.
o Modal rebate
This rebate is offered for paying the premium in the annual or half-yearly modes. Monthly and
quarterly modes might attract additional premiums but annual and half-yearly modes allow
discounts.
Moreover, under some plans you might also find lower premiums for females.
4. Evaluate plans before purchasing
There are different types of term insurance plans available in the market. Each plan is priced
differently. So, if you compare and then choose a suitable plan, you might enjoy affordable
premiums.
How to Avoid Rising Premium Costs?
Rising insurance premiums can impact your long-term financial planning. Here are some smart ways to keep your costs in check:
1. Select a Long-Term Fixed Premium Choose a term insurance plan with fixed premiums throughout the policy term. This locks your premium at the entry rate, protecting you from future hikes.
2. Review Your Coverage Frequently Review your policy every few years. You might be paying for more coverage than needed .
3. Improve Your Health Maintain a healthy lifestyle. Non-smokers, fit individuals, and those without pre-existing health conditions often get lower premium rates.
4. Compare Different Policies Always compare various policies and then only make your final decision. You can compare various plans as per your requirements.
How to Choose the Right Term Insurance Plan?
Choosing the right term insurance plan ensures financial security for your family. Here’s what to consider:
● Adequate Coverage Choose an assured sum that covers your family’s future needs, debts, and living expenses. A good rule is 10 times your annual income.
● Affordable Premium Select a plan with premiums that fit your budget while offering sufficient coverage. Avoid underinsuring just to save money.
● Riders and Add-Ons Look for essential riders like critical illness, accidental death benefit, or waiver of premium to enhance protection.
● Policy Term Pick a term that aligns with your retirement age or until major financial responsibilities are met.
● Insurer’s Credibility Check the insurance company’s claim settlement ratio and customer reviews for reliability and support. Making an informed choice gives you peace of mind and financial security for your loved ones.
Conclusion
Term insurance plans have low premiums which are affected by various factors. Know these
factors so that you know how the premium is calculated. Ensure that the premium is affordable
so that you can continue paying it over the chosen tenure and enjoy complete protection under
the term insurance plan.
FAQs
1. What is the term insurance premium, and why does it vary? A term insurance premium is the amount you pay regularly to keep your life insurance plan active. It varies based on your age, health, lifestyle, policy duration, and the coverage amount you choose amongst other factors.
2. How does age impact term insurance premiums? Age is a major factor in determining your premium. The younger you are when you buy term insurance, the lower your premium. This is because younger people are generally healthier and pose a lower risk to the insurer.
3. Will a change in the coverage amount impact my premium ?
Increasing the coverage amount will raise your premium, while reducing it can lower your premium. But make sure the coverage is still enough to secure your family's financial needs.
4. How does the policy duration affect the premium? Longer policy terms often have slightly higher premiums, but they offer protection for a longer time. Shorter terms may cost less, but could leave you without coverage when you need it later in life.
5. What are some of the factors that affect life insurance premiums? Premiums depend on various factors like your age, gender, health status, lifestyle habits (such as smoking), occupation, sum assured, policy term, and any added riders etc.
6. What does the term insurance premium depend on? Term insurance premium is calculated based on your profile (age, health, habits), the coverage amount, duration of the plan, and extra benefits or riders you choose. The higher the risk, the more you will pay.
7. Does the term insurance premium rise over time?
For most standard term insurance plans with a fixed tenure, the premium remains constant throughout
the policy term. However, in the case of renewable term plans (e.g., annual renewable term), the premium
may increase at the time of renewal based on your age and revised risk profile.
Disclaimer: The views stated in this article are not to be construed as investment advice and readers are suggested to seek independent financial advice before making any investment decisions. Yes, you can.
